mechanism_narrative: Sustained strength in the labor market, characterized by low unemployment and consistent job creation, indicates a healthy economy with robust consumer spending capacity. This fundamental strength drives corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:low unemployment (4.1%) and strong job growth (148M) as of 2018-03
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of periods where UNRATE is below 5% and PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate rises above a critical threshold, it signals an aggressive monetary tightening cycle by the central bank. This increases the cost of capital, reduces corporate profitability, and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales, when combined with persistently rising consumer price inflation, indicate strong consumer demand but also suggest an overheating economy. This scenario often forces the central bank to tighten monetary policy more aggressively, creating headwinds for equity markets.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods where both retail sales and inflation are rising above specified thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts during an economic expansion are a leading indicator of future economic activity and consumer confidence. This robust demand in the housing sector translates into broader economic growth, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of periods where HOUST is above its 12-month moving average during an expansionary regime.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration or outright decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity and broader economic slowdown. This directly impacts corporate revenues and earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investor sentiment sours.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite a negative 3-month change in industrial production.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation erodes the real value of corporate earnings and increases input costs, compressing profit margins. This inflationary pressure also raises the discount rate applied to future cash flows, leading to lower equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL is above its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: An extremely low unemployment rate combined with rising interest rates signals an economy that is potentially overheating, prompting the Federal Reserve to aggressively tighten monetary policy. This combination typically precedes a market correction as liquidity is withdrawn and growth expectations moderate.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% and FEDFUNDS at 1.51% as of 2018-03
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of periods where both UNRATE is below 4.5% and FEDFUNDS is above 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Robust real GDP growth signifies a healthy and expanding economy, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and profits. This strong fundamental backdrop boosts investor confidence and supports equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of periods where GDPC1 shows positive year-over-year growth within an expansionary regime.
